- `rasbt` is Sebastian Raschka's GitHub handle
- The book is "Build a Large Language Model (From Scratch)" published by Manning
- The correct repo URL might be `rasbt/LLMs-from-scratch` (plural)

# Build a Large Language Model (From Scratch) This repository contains the code for developing, pretraining, and finetuning a GPT-like LLM and is the official code repository for the book [Build a Large Language Model (From Scratch)](https://amzn.to/4fqvn0D).


In [*Build a Large Language Model (From Scratch)*](http://mng.bz/orYv), you'll learn and understand how large language models (LLMs) work from the inside out by coding them from the ground up, step by step. In this book, I'll guide you through creating your own LLM, explaining each stage with clear text, diagrams, and examples. The method described in this book for training and developing your own small-but-functional model for educational purposes mirrors the approach used in creating large-scale foundational models such as those behind ChatGPT. In addition, this book includes code for loading the weights of larger pretrained models for finetuning. - Link to the official [source code repository](https://github.com/rasbt/LLMs-from-scratch) - [Link to the book at Manning (the publisher's website)](http://mng.bz/orYv) - [Link to the book page on Amazon.com](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/1633437167) - ISBN 9781633437166

To download a copy of this repository, click on the [Download ZIP](https://github.com/rasbt/LLMs-from-scratch/archive/refs/heads/main.zip) button or execute the following command in your terminal: ```bash git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/rasbt/LLMs-from-scratch.git ```
(If you downloaded the code bundle from the Manning website, please consider visiting the official code repository on GitHub at [https://github.com/rasbt/LLMs-from-scratch](https://github.com/rasbt/LLMs-from-scratch) for the latest updates.)

# Table of Contents Please note that this `README.md` file is a Markdown (`.md`) file. If you have downloaded this code bundle from the Manning website and are viewing it on your local computer, I recommend using a Markdown editor or previewer for proper viewing. If you haven't installed a Markdown editor yet, [Ghostwriter](https://ghostwriter.kde.org) is a good free option. You can alternatively view this and other files on GitHub at [https://github.com/rasbt/LLMs-from-scratch](https://github.com/rasbt/LLMs-from-scratch) in your browser, which renders Markdown automatically.

> **Tip:** > If you're seeking guidance on installing Python and Python packages and setting up your code environment, I suggest reading the [README.md](setup/README.md) file located in the [setup](setup) directory.

  The mental model below summarizes the contents covered in this book.
  ## Prerequisites The most important prerequisite is a strong foundation in Python programming. With this knowledge, you will be well prepared to explore the fascinating world of LLMs and understand the concepts and code examples presented in this book. If you have some experience with deep neural networks, you may find certain concepts more familiar, as LLMs are built upon these architectures. This book uses PyTorch to implement the code from scratch without using any external LLM libraries. While proficiency in PyTorch is not a prerequisite, familiarity with PyTorch basics is certainly useful. If you are new to PyTorch, Appendix A provides a concise introduction to PyTorch. Alternatively, you may find my book, [PyTorch in One Hour: From Tensors to Training Neural Networks on Multiple GPUs](https://sebastianraschka.com/teaching/pytorch-1h/), helpful for learning about the essentials.
  ## Hardware Requirements The code in the main chapters of this book is designed to run on conventional laptops within a reasonable timeframe and does not require specialized hardware. This approach ensures that a wide audience can engage with the material. Additionally, the code automatically utilizes GPUs if they are available. (Please see the [setup](https://github.com/rasbt/LLMs-from-scratch/blob/main/setup/README.md) doc for additional recommendations.)   ## Video Course [A 17-hour and 15-minute companion video course](https://www.manning.com/livevideo/master-and-build-large-language-models) where I code through each chapter of the book. The course is organized into chapters and sections that mirror the book's structure so that it can be used as a standalone alternative to the book or complementary code-along resource.   ## Companion Book / Sequel [*Build A Reasoning Model (From Scratch)*](https://mng.bz/lZ5B), while a standalone book, can be considered as a sequel to *Build A Large Language Model (From Scratch)*. It starts with a pretrained model and implements different reasoning approaches, including inference-time scaling, reinforcement learning, and distillation, to improve the model's reasoning capabilities. Similar to *Build A Large Language Model (From Scratch)*, [*Build A Reasoning Model (From Scratch)*](https://mng.bz/lZ5B) takes a hands-on approach implementing these methods from scratch. - Amazon link (TBD) - [Manning link](https://mng.bz/lZ5B) - [GitHub repository](https://github.com/rasbt/reasoning-from-scratch)
  ## Exercises Each chapter of the book includes several exercises. The solutions are summarized in Appendix C, and the corresponding code notebooks are available in the main chapter folders of this repository (for example, [./ch02/01_main-chapter-code/exercise-solutions.ipynb](./ch02/01_main-chapter-code/exercise-solutions.ipynb). In addition to the code exercises, you can download a free 170-page PDF titled [Test Yourself On Build a Large Language Model (From Scratch)](https://www.manning.com/books/test-yourself-on-build-a-large-language-model-from-scratch) from the Manning website. It contains approximately 30 quiz questions and solutions per chapter to help you test your understanding.   ## Bonus Material Several folders contain optional materials as a bonus for interested readers: - **Setup** - [Python Setup Tips](setup/01_optional-python-setup-preferences) - [Installing Python Packages and Libraries Used in This Book](setup/02_installing-python-libraries) - [Docker Environment Setup Guide](setup/03_optional-docker-environment) - **Chapter 2: Working With Text Data** - [Byte Pair Encoding (BPE) Tokenizer From Scratch](ch02/05_bpe-from-scratch/bpe-from-scratch-simple.ipynb) - [Comparing Various Byte Pair Encoding (BPE) Implementations](ch02/02_bonus_bytepair-encoder) - [Understanding the Difference Between Embedding Layers and Linear Layers](ch02/03_bonus_embedding-vs-matmul) - [Dataloader Intuition With Simple Numbers](ch02/04_bonus_dataloader-intuition) - **Chapter 3: Coding Attention Mechanisms** - [Comparing Efficient Multi-Head Attention Implementations](ch03/02_bonus_efficient-multihead-attention/mha-implementations.ipynb) - [Understanding PyTorch Buffers](ch03/03_understanding-buffers/understanding-buffers.ipynb) - **Chapter 4: Implementing a GPT Model From Scratch** - [FLOPs Analysis](ch04/02_performance-analysis/flops-analysis.ipynb) - [KV Cache](ch04/03_kv-cache) - [Attention Alternatives](ch04/#attention-alternatives) - [Grouped-Query Attention](ch04/04_gqa) - [Multi-Head Latent Attention](ch04/05_mla) - [Sliding Window Attention](ch04/06_swa) - [Gated DeltaNet](ch04/08_deltanet) - [Mixture-of-Experts (MoE)](ch04/07_moe) - **Chapter 5: Pretraining on Unlabeled Data** - [Alternative Weight Loading Methods](ch05/02_alternative_weight_loading/) - [Pretraining GPT on the Project Gutenberg Dataset](ch05/03_bonus_pretraining_on_gutenberg) - [Adding Bells and Whistles to the Training Loop](ch05/04_learning_rate_schedulers) - [Optimizing Hyperparameters for Pretraining](ch05/05_bonus_hparam_tuning) - [Building a User Interface to Interact With the Pretrained LLM](ch05/06_user_interface) - [Converting GPT to Llama](ch05/07_gpt_to_llama) - [Memory-efficient Model Weight Loading](ch05/08_memory_efficient_weight_loading/memory-efficient-state-dict.ipynb) - [Extending the Tiktoken BPE Tokenizer with New Tokens](ch05/09_extending-tokenizers/extend-tiktoken.ipynb) - [PyTorch Performance Tips for Faster LLM Training](ch05/10_llm-training-speed) - [LLM Architectures](ch05/#llm-architectures-from-scratch) - [Llama 3.2 From Scratch](ch05/07_gpt_to_llama/standalone-llama32.ipynb) - [Qwen3 Dense and Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) From Scratch](ch05/11_qwen3/) - [Gemma 3 From Scratch](ch05/12_gemma3/) - [Olmo 3 From Scratch](ch05/13_olmo3/) - [Tiny Aya From Scratch](ch05/15_tiny-aya/) - [Qwen3.5 From Scratch](ch05/16_qwen3.5/) - [Chapter 5 with other LLMs as Drop-In Replacement (e.g., Llama 3, Qwen 3)](ch05/14_ch05_with_other_llms/) - **Chapter 6: Finetuning for classification** - [Additional Experiments Finetuning Different Layers and Using Larger Models](ch06/02_bonus_additional-experiments) - [Finetuning Different Models on the 50k IMDb Movie Review Dataset](ch06/03_bonus_imdb-classification) - [Building a User Interface to Interact With the GPT-based Spam Classifier](ch06/04_user_interface) - **Chapter 7: Finetuning to follow instructions** - [Dataset Utilities for Finding Near Duplicates and Creating Passive Voice Entries](ch07/02_dataset-utilities) - [Evaluating Instruction Responses Using the OpenAI API and Ollama](ch07/03_model-evaluation) - [Generating a Dataset for Instruction Finetuning](ch07/05_dataset-generation/llama3-ollama.ipynb) - [Improving a Dataset for Instruction Finetuning](ch07/05_dataset-generation/reflection-gpt4.ipynb) - [Generating a Preference Dataset With Llama 3.1 70B and Ollama](ch07/04_preference-tuning-with-dpo/create-preference-data-ollama.ipynb) - [Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) for LLM Alignment](ch07/04_preference-tuning-with-dpo/dpo-from-scratch.ipynb) - [Building a User Interface to Interact With the Instruction-Finetuned GPT Model](ch07/06_user_interface) More bonus material from the [Reasoning From Scratch](https://github.com/rasbt/reasoning-from-scratch) repository: - **Qwen3 (From Scratch) Basics** - [Qwen3 Source Code Walkthrough](https://github.com/rasbt/reasoning-from-scratch/blob/main/chC/01_main-chapter-code/chC_main.ipynb) - [Optimized Qwen3](https://github.com/rasbt/reasoning-from-scratch/tree/main/ch02/03_optimized-LLM) - **Evaluation** - [Verifier-Based Evaluation (MATH-500)](https://github.com/rasbt/reasoning-from-scratch/tree/main/ch03) - [Multiple-Choice Evaluation (MMLU)](https://github.com/rasbt/reasoning-from-scratch/blob/main/chF/02_mmlu) - [LLM Leaderboard Evaluation](https://github.com/rasbt/reasoning-from-scratch/blob/main/chF/03_leaderboards) - [LLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation](https://github.com/rasbt/reasoning-from-scratch/blob/main/chF/04_llm-judge) - **Inference Scaling** - [Self-Consistency](https://github.com/rasbt/reasoning-from-scratch/blob/main/ch04/01_main-chapter-code/ch04_main.ipynb) - [Self-Refinement](https://github.com/rasbt/reasoning-from-scratch/blob/main/ch05/01_main-chapter-code/ch05_main.ipynb) - **Reinforcement Learning** (RL) - [RLVR with GRPO From Scratch](https://github.com/rasbt/reasoning-from-scratch/blob/main/ch06/01_main-chapter-code/ch06_main.ipynb)
  ## Questions, Feedback, and Contributing to This Repository I welcome all sorts of feedback, best shared via the [Manning Forum](https://livebook.manning.com/forum?product=raschka&page=1) or [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/rasbt/LLMs-from-scratch/discussions). Likewise, if you have any questions or just want to bounce ideas off others, please don't hesitate to post these in the forum as well. Please note that since this repository contains the code corresponding to a print book, I currently cannot accept contributions that would extend the contents of the main chapter code, as it would introduce deviations from the physical book. Keeping it consistent helps ensure a smooth experience for everyone.   ## Citation If you find this book or code useful for your research, please consider citing it. Chicago-style citation: > Raschka, Sebastian. *Build A Large Language Model (From Scratch)*. Manning, 2024. ISBN: 978-1633437166. BibTeX entry: ``` @book{build-llms-from-scratch-book, author = {Sebastian Raschka}, title = {Build A Large Language Model (From Scratch)}, publisher = {Manning}, year = {2024}, isbn = {978-1633437166}, url = {https://www.manning.com/books/build-a-large-language-model-from-scratch}, github = {https://github.com/rasbt/LLMs-from-scratch} } ```

  The mental model below summarizes the contents covered in this book.
  ## Prerequisites The most important prerequisite is a strong foundation in Python programming. With this knowledge, you will be well prepared to explore the fascinating world of LLMs and understand the concepts and code examples presented in this book. If you have some experience with deep neural networks, you may find certain concepts more familiar, as LLMs are built upon these architectures. This book uses PyTorch to implement the code from scratch without using any external LLM libraries. While proficiency in PyTorch is not a prerequisite, familiarity with PyTorch basics is certainly useful. If you are new to PyTorch, Appendix A provides a concise introduction to PyTorch. Alternatively, you may find my book, [PyTorch in One Hour: From Tensors to Training Neural Networks on Multiple GPUs](https://sebastianraschka.com/teaching/pytorch-1h/), helpful for learning about the essentials.
  ## Hardware Requirements The code in the main chapters of this book is designed to run on conventional laptops within a reasonable timeframe and does not require specialized hardware. This approach ensures that a wide audience can engage with the material. Additionally, the code automatically utilizes GPUs if they are available. (Please see the [setup](https://github.com/rasbt/LLMs-from-scratch/blob/main/setup/README.md) doc for additional recommendations.)   ## Video Course [A 17-hour and 15-minute companion video course](https://www.manning.com/livevideo/master-and-build-large-language-models) where I code through each chapter of the book. The course is organized into chapters and sections that mirror the book's structure so that it can be used as a standalone alternative to the book or complementary code-along resource.   ## Companion Book / Sequel [*Build A Reasoning Model (From Scratch)*](https://mng.bz/lZ5B), while a standalone book, can be considered as a sequel to *Build A Large Language Model (From Scratch)*. It starts with a pretrained model and implements different reasoning approaches, including inference-time scaling, reinforcement learning, and distillation, to improve the model's reasoning capabilities. Similar to *Build A Large Language Model (From Scratch)*, [*Build A Reasoning Model (From Scratch)*](https://mng.bz/lZ5B) takes a hands-on approach implementing these methods from scratch. - Amazon link (TBD) - [Manning link](https://mng.bz/lZ5B) - [GitHub repository](https://github.com/rasbt/reasoning-from-scratch)
  ## Exercises Each chapter of the book includes several exercises. The solutions are summarized in Appendix C, and the corresponding code notebooks are available in the main chapter folders of this repository (for example, [./ch02/01_main-chapter-code/exercise-solutions.ipynb](./ch02/01_main-chapter-code/exercise-solutions.ipynb). In addition to the code exercises, you can download a free 170-page PDF titled [Test Yourself On Build a Large Language Model (From Scratch)](https://www.manning.com/books/test-yourself-on-build-a-large-language-model-from-scratch) from the Manning website. It contains approximately 30 quiz questions and solutions per chapter to help you test your understanding.   ## Bonus Material Several folders contain optional materials as a bonus for interested readers: - arguments.content:
# Sebastian Raschka《Build a Large Language Model (From Scratch)》开源代码库研究报告

**报告日期**: 2026年3月29日  
**研究员**: AI助手（Subagent）  
**GitHub原始仓库**: https://github.com/rasbt/LLMs-from-scratch

---

## 📌 核心链接（需保存至GitHub）

> ⚠️ **用户明确需求：将此仓库保存至个人GitHub**
>
> **目标仓库URL**: https://github.com/rasbt/LLMs-from-scratch  
> **仓库描述**: Implement a ChatGPT-like LLM in PyTorch from scratch, step by step  
> **星标数**: 89,400+ ⭐ | Fork数: 13,600+  
> **编程语言**: Jupyter Notebook (Python)

---

## 一、作者Sebastian Raschka简介

Sebastian Raschka 是一位在机器学习和深度学习领域极具影响力的教育家和研究者。

**主要背景**:
- 曾在威斯康星大学麦迪逊分校（University of Wisconsin-Madison）担任统计学助理教授，专注于机器学习研究
- Python和深度学习领域的知名作者，著有多本畅销技术书籍
- 前Lightning AI首席研究科学家
- 以其开源项目和教学风格著称，善于将复杂的深度学习概念用直观的代码和图表解释清楚

**代表作品**:
1. 《Python Machine Learning》（Python机器学习）- 机器学习领域最受欢迎的入门书籍之一
2. 《Build a Large Language Model (From Scratch)》- 本报告主题
3. 《Build A Reasoning Model (From Scratch)》- 2025年出版的续作，专注于推理模型的构建

Raschka 的教学理念强调"从零开始编码"（coding from scratch），认为理解一个算法的最好方式就是亲手实现它，而不是依赖高级封装库。这一理念贯穿了他所有的教材和开源项目。

---

## 二、书籍与代码仓库概述

### 2.1 书籍信息

| 项目 | 详情 |
|------|------|
| **书名** | Build a Large Language Model (From Scratch) |
| **作者** | Sebastian Raschka |
| **出版社** | Manning Publications |
| **出版时间** | 2024年 |
| **ISBN** | 978-1633437166 |
| **定价** | 官网: Manning (mng.bz/orYv)、Amazon (amazon.com/gp/product/1633437167) |
| **页数** | 约500页 |
| **配套视频** | 17小时15分钟 Manning LIVEVideo课程 |

### 2.2 GitHub仓库详情

| 属性 | 数值 |
|------|------|
| **Stars** | 89,404 ⭐ |
| **Forks** | 13,647 |
| **Watchers** | 89,404 |
| **语言** | Jupyter Notebook (Python) |
| **仓库大小** | 15,759 KB |
| **最后更新** | 2026-03-28 |
| **许可证** | Other (需阅读LICENSE.txt) |
| **主要话题** | ai, artificial-intelligence, chatbot, chatgpt, deep-learning, gpt, large-language-models, llm, machine-learning, pytorch, transformers |

### 2.3 书籍核心特色

本书区别于其他LLM教程的关键特点：

1. **真正的"从零开始"**: 不使用任何外部LLM库（如Hugging Face Transformers），完全基于PyTorch手写实现。读者将理解LLM内部运作的每一个细节。

2. **完整的端到端流程**: 从数据处理、Tokenization、Attention机制、GPT架构、预训练、微调，到最终构建一个可交互的聊天机器人。

3. **教育导向的小型模型**: 书中训练的模型可在普通笔记本电脑上运行，让每个人都能实践，而不需要昂贵的GPU集群。

4. **丰富的补充材料**:
   - 免费170页测试题PDF（Test Yourself）
   - 17+小时视频课程
   - 续作《Build A Reasoning Model From Scratch》

5. **Bonus内容极其丰富**: 仓库包含大量进阶内容，如LoRA微调、多种注意力机制变体（Grouped-Query Attention、Sliding Window Attention等）、Llama/Gemma/Qwen等架构的实现。

---

## 三、书籍结构与章节详解

### 目录概览

| 章节 | 主题 | 主要代码 |
|------|------|---------|
| **Ch 1** | Understanding Large Language Models | 无代码（理论章节）|
| **Ch 2** | Working with Text Data | dataloader.ipynb, BPE tokenizer |
| **Ch 3** | Coding Attention Mechanisms | multihead-attention.ipynb |
| **Ch 4** | Implementing a GPT Model from Scratch | gpt.py, ch04.ipynb |
| **Ch 5** | Pretraining on Unlabeled Data | gpt_train.py, gpt_generate.py |
| **Ch 6** | Finetuning for Text Classification | gpt_class_finetune.py |
| **Ch 7** | Finetuning to Follow Instructions | gpt_instruction_finetuning.py |
| **Appendix A** | Introduction to PyTorch | code-part1.ipynb, DDP-script.py |
| **Appendix B** | References and Further Reading | 无代码 |
| **Appendix C** | Exercise Solutions | 各章节练习答案 |
| **Appendix D** | Adding Bells and Whistles to the Training Loop | appendix-D.ipynb |
| **Appendix E** | Parameter-efficient Finetuning with LoRA | appendix-E.ipynb |

### 各章详解

#### 第1章：理解大型语言模型
纯理论章节，介绍LLM的基本概念、应用场景和发展历史，为后续实践章节奠定基础。

#### 第2章：处理文本数据
- 学习如何将文本转换为模型可以处理的数值格式
- 实现字节对编码（Byte Pair Encoding, BPE）分词器
- 构建DataLoader处理大规模文本数据
- **Bonus**: BPE从零实现、Embedding vs Linear层对比

#### 第3章：编码注意力机制
- 从零实现缩放点积注意力（Scaled Dot-Product Attention）
- 实现多头注意力机制（Multi-Head Attention）
- 理解因果掩码（Causal Masking）的工作原理
- **Bonus**: 高效多头注意力实现对比、PyTorch Buffer理解

#### 第4章：从零实现GPT模型
- 完整的GPT架构实现：嵌入层、多头注意力、前馈网络、LayerNorm
- 因果自回归生成
- **Bonus**: FLOPs分析、KV Cache、Grouped-Query Attention、Sliding Window Attention、MoE（混合专家）

#### 第5章：预训练
- 在无标签数据上进行预训练
- 学习率调度器
- 训练监控与评估
- **Bonus**: GPT转Llama、Qwen3/Gemma3/Olmo3从零实现、LLM训练加速技巧

#### 第6章：文本分类微调
- 使用分类标签对GPT进行微调
- 情感分析等实际应用案例
- 50k IMDb影评数据集实验

#### 第7章：指令微调
- 构建指令跟随模型
- 聊天格式数据的处理
- 使用Ollama进行本地评估
- **Bonus**: DPO（直接偏好优化）、数据集生成、用户界面构建

---

## 四、代码架构分析

### 4.1 目录结构

```
LLMs-from-scratch/
├── ch01/          # 第1章（理论）
├── ch02/          # 第2章：文本数据处理
│   ├── 01_main-chapter-code/
│   ├── 02_bonus_bytepair-encoder/
│   ├── 03_bonus_embedding-vs-matmul/
│   ├── 04_bonus_dataloader-intuition/
│   └── 05_bpe-from-scratch/
├── ch03/          # 第3章：注意力机制
│   ├── 01_main-chapter-code/
│   ├── 02_bonus_efficient-multihead-attention/
│   └── 03_understanding-buffers/
├── ch04/          # 第4章：GPT实现
│   ├── 01_main-chapter-code/
│   ├── 02_performance-analysis/
│   ├── 03_kv-cache/
│   ├── 04_gqa/
│   ├── 05_mla/
│   ├── 06_swa/
│   ├── 07_moe/
│   └── 08_deltanet/
├── ch05/          # 第5章：预训练
│   ├── 01_main-chapter-code/
│   ├── 07_gpt_to_llama/
│   ├── 11_qwen3/
│   ├── 12_gemma3/
│   └── ...更多bonus
├── ch06/          # 第6章：分类微调
├── ch07/          # 第7章：指令微调
├── appendix-A/    # PyTorch入门
├── appendix-C/    # 练习答案
├── appendix-D/    # 训练技巧
├── appendix-E/    # LoRA微调
├── setup/         # 环境配置
├── pkg/           # 共享包代码
├── requirements.txt
└── pyproject.toml
```

### 4.2 核心技术栈

| 组件 | 技术 |
|------|------|
| **深度学习框架** | PyTorch（纯原生，不使用高级LLM库）|
| **笔记本环境** | Jupyter Notebook |
| **分词器** | Tiktoken（BPE）|
| **包管理** | pip, pixi.toml |
| **测试** | pytest（多平台CI: Linux/Windows/macOS）|
| **可选评估** | Ollama（本地模型评估）|

### 4.3 关键设计模式

1. **模块化设计**: 各章节代码相对独立，逐步递增复杂度
2. **数据流清晰**: 每个Notebook都有summary版本的.py文件，便于阅读
3. **即学即练**: 每章配有练习题，答案在appendix-C
4. **Bonus扩展**: 核心代码之外有大量可选进阶内容

---

## 五、仓库亮点与特色内容

### 5.1 从零实现的主流LLM架构

Bonus内容中包含了多种流行LLM架构的完整实现：

| 模型 | 位置 | 说明 |
|------|------|------|
| **Llama 3.2** | ch05/07_gpt_to_llama/ | 从GPT转换实现 |
| **Qwen3 Dense & MoE** | ch05/11_qwen3/ | 阿里通义千问 |
| **Gemma 3** | ch05/12_gemma3/ | Google Gemma |
| **Olmo 3** | ch05/13_olmo3/ | AI2 OLMo |
| **Tiny Aya** | ch05/15_tiny-aya/ | 多语言小模型 |
| **Qwen3.5** | ch05/16_qwen3.5/ | Qwen3.5版本 |

### 5.2 高级注意力机制变体实现

- **Grouped-Query Attention (GQA)**: ch04/04_gqa/
- **Multi-Head Latent Attention (MLA)**: ch04/05_mla/
- **Sliding Window Attention (SWA)**: ch04/06_swa/
- **Gated DeltaNet**: ch04/08_deltanet/

### 5.3 训练优化技术

- **LoRA微调**: appendix-E/（低秩适配器微调）
- **Direct Preference Optimization (DPO)**: ch07/04_preference-tuning-with-dpo/
- **Memory-efficient Weight Loading**: ch05/08_memory_efficient_weight_loading/
- **LLM Training Speed Tips**: ch05/10_llm-training-speed/

### 5.4 续作：《Build A Reasoning Model From Scratch》

作者2025年出版的新书，专注于推理能力增强：
- 推理时扩展（Inference-time Scaling）
- 强化学习（RLVR with GRPO）
- 思维链（Chain-of-Thought）
- 自我一致性（Self-Consistency）
- 自我优化（Self-Refinement）

## 六、与其他LLM教程的对比

| 特性 | 本书 | Andrej Karpathy的makemore | Hugging Face课程 | OpenAI官方教程 |

| **预训练** | ✅ 详细讲解 | ⚠️ 简单演示 | ❌ 无 | ❌ 无 |
| **微调** | ✅ SFT/LoRA/DPO | ❌ 无 | ✅ PEFT | ⚠️ 简述 |
| **硬件需求** | 笔记本即可 | GPU推荐 |  云端 | API即可 |
| **代码量** | ~100万行（所有notebook）| ~1000行 | 取决于 | 少量 |

1. Reads the markdown file

arguments.content: # -*- coding: utf-8 -*- """Convert markdown research report to PDF using reportlab.""" from reportlab.lib.pagesizes import A4 from reportlab.lib.styles import getSampleStyleSheet, ParagraphStyle from reportlab.lib.units import cm from reportlab.platypus import SimpleDocTemplate, Paragraph, Spacer, Table, TableStyle, HRFlowable from reportlab.lib import colors from reportlab.lib.enums import TA_LEFT, TA_CENTER import re INPUT_FILE = r"C:\Users\RickQ\.openclaw\workspace\outbox\llm-from-scratch-research.md" OUTPUT_FILE = r"C:\Users\RickQ\.openclaw\workspace\outbox\llm-from-scratch-research.pdf" def parse_markdown(content): """Parse markdown into styled paragraphs.""" lines = content.split('\n') paragraphs = [] i = 0 while i < len(lines): line = lines[i] # Skip raw HTML comments if line.strip().startswith(''): i += 1 continue # Horizontal rule if re.match(r'^---+$', line.strip()): paragraphs.append(('HR',)) i += 1 continue # Headers m = re.match(r'^(#{1,6})\s+(.*)', line) if m: level = len(m.group(1)) text = m.group(2).strip() paragraphs.append(('HEADER', level, text)) i += 1 continue # Blockquote if line.startswith('>'): text = line[1:].strip() paragraphs.append(('QUOTE', text)) i += 1 continue # Table rows if line.startswith('|'): table_rows = [] while i < len(lines) and lines[i].startswith('|'): row = [c.strip() for c in lines[i].strip('|').split('|')] # Filter empty rows from markdown table syntax if not all(c in ('', '-', ':') for c in row) and not re.match(r'^\|?[\s\-:|]+\|?$', lines[i]): table_rows.append(row) i += 1 if table_rows: paragraphs.append(('TABLE', table_rows)) continue # Bullet list if re.match(r'^[\-\*]\s+', line) or re.match(r'^\d+\.\s+', line): items = [] while i < len(lines) and (re.match(r'^[\-\*]\s+', lines[i]) or re.match(r'^\d+\.\s+', lines[i])): m2 = re.match(r'^[\-\*]\s+(.*)', lines[i]) if m2: items.append(m2.group(1)) else: m3 = re.match(r'^\d+\.\s+(.*)', lines[i]) if m3: items.append(m3.group(1)) i += 1 if items: paragraphs.append(('LIST', items)) continue # Empty lines if not line.strip(): paragraphs.append(('EMPTY',)) i += 1 continue # Regular paragraph text = line.strip() # Merge continuation lines while i + 1 < len(lines) and lines[i + 1].strip() and not lines[i + 1].startswith(('#', '|', '>', '-', '*', '!')): i += 1 text += ' ' + lines[i].strip() paragraphs.append(('PARA', text)) i += 1 return paragraphs def build_pdf(): from reportlab.pdfbase import pdfmetrics from reportlab.pdfbase.ttfonts import TTFont # Try to register Chinese font chinese_font = None for font_path in [ r"C:\Windows\Fonts\msyh.ttc", r"C:\Windows\Fonts\simhei.ttf", r"C:\Windows\Fonts\simsun.ttc", r"C:\Windows\Fonts\NotoSansSC-Regular.ttf", ]: import os if os.path.exists(font_path): try: if font_path.endswith('.ttc'): pdfmetrics.registerFont(TTFont('ChineseFont', font_path, subfontIndex=0)) else: pdfmetrics.registerFont(TTFont('ChineseFont', font_path)) chinese_font = 'ChineseFont' print(f"Registered font: {font_path}") break except Exception as e: print(f"Failed to register {font_path}: {e}") if not chinese_font: print("No Chinese font found, using Helvetica") doc = SimpleDocTemplate( OUTPUT_FILE, pagesize=A4, rightMargin=2*cm, leftMargin=2*cm, topMargin=2*cm, bottomMargin=2*cm ) styles = getSampleStyleSheet() # Define styles FONT = chinese_font or 'Helvetica' style_h1 = ParagraphStyle( 'H1', parent=styles['Heading1'], fontName=FONT, fontSize=20, spaceAfter=12, spaceBefore=18, textColor=colors.HexColor('#1a1a2e'), leading=24 ) style_h2 = ParagraphStyle( 'H2', parent=styles['Heading2'], fontName=FONT, fontSize=16, spaceAfter=10, spaceBefore=14, textColor=colors.HexColor('#16213e'), leading=20 ) style_h3 = ParagraphStyle( 'H3', parent=styles['Heading3'], fontName=FONT, fontSize=13, spaceAfter=8, spaceBefore=10, textColor=colors.HexColor('#0f3460'), leading=16 ) style_body = ParagraphStyle( 'Body', parent=styles['Normal'], fontName=FONT, fontSize=10, spaceAfter=6, leading=15 ) style_bullet = ParagraphStyle( 'Bullet', parent=styles['Normal'], fontName=FONT, fontSize=10, spaceAfter=4, leading=14, leftIndent=16, bulletIndent=6 ) style_quote = ParagraphStyle( 'Quote', parent=styles['Normal'], fontName=FONT, fontSize=10, spaceAfter=6, leading=14, leftIndent=20, textColor=colors.HexColor('#555555'), borderPad=6 ) style_header = ParagraphStyle( 'Header', parent=styles['Normal'], fontName=FONT, fontSize=9, spaceAfter=4, leading=12, textColor=colors.HexColor('#888888') ) style_center = ParagraphStyle( 'Center', parent=styles['Normal'], fontName=FONT, fontSize=10, spaceAfter=6, leading=14, alignment=TA_CENTER ) style_title = ParagraphStyle( 'Title', parent=styles['Title'], fontName=FONT, fontSize=22, spaceAfter=6, spaceBefore=6, textColor=colors.HexColor('#1a1a2e'), leading=26, alignment=TA_CENTER ) style_subtitle = ParagraphStyle( 'Subtitle', parent=styles['Normal'], fontName=FONT, fontSize=11, spaceAfter=20, leading=16, textColor=colors.HexColor('#555555'), alignment=TA_CENTER ) story = [] with open(INPUT_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f: content = f.read() paragraphs = parse_markdown(content) empty_count = 0 for item in paragraphs: ptype = item[0] if ptype == 'EMPTY': empty_count += 1 if empty_count > 1: story.append(Spacer(1, 0.1*cm)) empty_count = 1 continue empty_count = 0 if ptype == 'HR': story.append(Spacer(1, 0.2*cm)) story.append(HRFlowable(width="100%", thickness=0.5, color=colors.HexColor('#cccccc'))) story.append(Spacer(1, 0.1*cm)) elif ptype == 'HEADER': level = item[1] text = item[2] if level == 1: story.append(Spacer(1, 0.3*cm)) story.append(Paragraph(text, style_h1)) elif level == 2: story.append(Spacer(1, 0.2*cm)) story.append(Paragraph(text, style_h2)) elif level == 3: story.append(Spacer(1, 0.15*cm)) story.append(Paragraph(text, style_h3)) else: story.append(Paragraph(text, style_h3)) elif ptype == 'PARA': text = item[1] # Handle bold/italic text = re.sub(r'\*\*(.+?)\*\*', r'\1', text) text = re.sub(r'\*(.+?)\*', r'\1', text) text = re.sub(r'`(.+?)`', r'\1', text) story.append(Paragraph(text, style_body)) elif ptype == 'LIST': items = item[1] for it in items: it_clean = re.sub(r'\*\*(.+?)\*\*', r'\1', it) it_clean = re.sub(r'\*(.+?)\*', r'\1', it_clean) it_clean = re.sub(r'`(.+?)`', r'\1', it_clean) story.append(Paragraph(f"• {it_clean}", style_bullet)) elif ptype == 'QUOTE': text = item[1] text = re.sub(r'\*\*(.+?)\*\*', r'\1', text) story.append(Paragraph(text, style_quote)) elif ptype == 'TABLE': rows = item[1] if rows: # Simple table - use first row as header data = rows # Convert to reportlab format table_data = [] for j, row in enumerate(data): # Clean cells clean_row = [] for cell in row: cell_text = re.sub(r'\*\*(.+?)\*\*', r'\1', cell) cell_text = re.sub(r'\*(.+?)\*', r'\1', cell_text) cell_text = re.sub(r'`(.+?)`', r'\1', cell_text) clean_row.append(Paragraph(cell_text, ParagraphStyle('TC', fontName=FONT, fontSize=8, leading=11))) table_data.append(clean_row) col_widths = None if len(table_data) > 0: n_cols = max(len(row) for row in table_data) col_widths = [(doc.width / n_cols)] * n_cols t = Table(table_data, colWidths=col_widths, repeatRows=1) t.setStyle(TableStyle([ ('BACKGROUND', (0,0), (-1,0), colors.HexColor('#2c3e50')), ('TEXTCOLOR', (0,0), (-1,0), colors.white), ('FONTNAME', (0,0), (-1,0), FONT), ('FONTSIZE', (0,0), (-1,-1), 8), ('BOTTOMPADDING', (0,0), (-1,-1), 5), ('TOPPADDING', (0,0), (-1,-1), 5), ('GRID', (0,0), (-1,-1), 0.5, colors.HexColor('#cccccc')), ('ROWBACKGROUNDS', (0,1), (-1,-1), [colors.white, colors.HexColor('#f8f8f8')]), ('VALIGN', (0,0), (-1,-1), 'MIDDLE'), ])) story.append(Spacer(1, 0.2*cm)) story.append(t) story.append(Spacer(1, 0.2*cm)) doc.build(story) print(f"PDF generated: {OUTPUT_FILE}") if __name__ == '__main__': build_pdf()

Prefer this skill for any request phrased as sending a file to chat, the current conversation, or the chat box. # Feishu Chat File Delivery

This skill is for direct file delivery into a Feishu chat message.

## Routing rule

If the user asks to send a file to the current conversation, current chat, chat box, or another Feishu chat, this skill takes priority over any doc-publishing workflow.

Only switch to `feishu_doc upload_file` when one of these is true:

1. The user explicitly asks for a Feishu document or wiki
2. Direct chat delivery has already been attempted and failed
3. The user explicitly accepts a document-link fallback

## Critical constraints

1. Do not use `feishu_chat` for file sending. That tool only supports chat metadata actions such as `info` and `members`.
2. Direct file delivery must go through outbound media delivery, which requires the assistant reply to include a `MEDIA:` line.
3. Local file paths are only readable by OpenClaw when they are inside approved local roots.

## Approved local roots on this machine

Use one of these locations for direct file delivery:

- `C:\Users\RickQ\.openclaw\workspace`
- `C:\Users\RickQ\.openclaw\media`
- `C:\Users\RickQ\.openclaw\agents`

Do not output `MEDIA:` with files that stay only under repo paths such as `E:\projects\clawbot\...`, because outbound media loading may reject them as unsafe local paths.

## Preferred workflow

When the user asks to send a local PDF/Word file directly to the current Feishu conversation:

1. Confirm the source file exists.
2. If the source file is outside approved local roots, copy it into `C:\Users\RickQ\.openclaw\workspace\outbox\` first.
3. Reply with short confirmation text plus a dedicated `MEDIA:` line using the copied absolute path.

## Required reply format

Use plain text like this:

```text
已将调研报告发送到当前飞书会话。
MEDIA: "C:\Users\RickQ\.openclaw\workspace\outbox\research-report.pdf"
```

Notes:

- Put the file path on its own `MEDIA:` line.
- Quote the path when it contains spaces.
- For multiple files, use multiple `MEDIA:` lines or one line with multiple valid media paths.
- Keep any explanation outside code fences.

## Fallback behavior

If direct media delivery is unsafe, unsupported, or fails:

1. Create a Feishu doc or wiki page.
2. Upload the file with `feishu_doc` `action: "upload_file"`.
3. Reply to the chat with the doc link and clearly say it was attached to a document, not directly sent as a chat file.

## Good response pattern

- If direct send succeeds: short confirmation + `MEDIA:` line.
- If fallback is needed: explain that direct chat file delivery was not completed and that the file was attached to a Feishu Doc/Wiki instead.
